insights.insight: A verified receipt is a confirmed purchase event, not an inference. The ad industry currently pays $5–$150 CPA for probabilistic attribution that this protocol replaces with cryptographic proof at roughly $0.10 per verified conversion.

With Chrome joining Safari and Firefox in deprecating cross-site tracking, the primary probabilistic targeting primitive is gone. Verified receipt data is identity-free and doesn't depend on cookies, fingerprinting, or modeled conversions — it survives the deprecation entirely.

Amazon sees Amazon. Walmart sees Walmart. No retailer or platform sees the full consumer purchase record — except the email inbox, which receives receipts from every vendor. This protocol is the only known method converting that cross-ecosystem record into a queryable, identity-free dataset.

4,001 verified receipts representing $233,481 in GMV took months to build. DKIM verification is 20-year-old technology — the defensible asset is the accumulated dataset, not the pipeline. Any competitor starts at zero.

27 active CBSAs now include non-metro markets like rural Mountain West, suggesting the dataset is expanding beyond urban early adopters toward a nationally representative household spend picture — which matters for brand marketers evaluating reach.

Five pending vendor requests are all developer infrastructure (Vercel, Render, DigitalOcean, Hetzner) — not physical retail. If approved, this would be the first verified dataset combining physical grocery spend and digital subscription spend in a single identity-free corpus.
